Title: OROSOMUCOID PROTEIN 1 regulation of sphingolipid synthesis is required for nodulation in Aeschynomene evenia

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OROSOMUCOID/ORM family protein; small highly conserved ER membrane protein with four predicted transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; enriched in lateral root primordia, base and tip of elongating lateral roots, nodule primordium initiation sites at lateral root bases, nodule base and peripheral cell layers surrounding infected tissue
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lateral root initiation and elongation; lateral root bases during nodulation; mature nodules at base/periphery
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains sphingolipid homeostasis by preventing ceramide overaccumulation; required for normal lateral root elongation and rhizobial infection/nodule organogenesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sphingolipids are membrane structural and bioactive lipids important for vesicular trafficking, cell polarization, cytokinesis, tissue patterning, and symbiotic accommodation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EMS mutations in AeORM1 caused aborted brown nodule-like bumps, nitrogen starvation symptoms, and loss of functional nodules. 2. Complementation with ProAeORM1:AeORM1 restored nodulation. 3. Sphingolipidomics showed orm1 mutants overaccumulate ceramides, particularly trihydroxy long-chain-base ceramides with VLCFA chains (C22-C26). 4. AeORM1 is expressed in developing lateral roots and nodule initiation zones, linking sphingolipid homeostasis to lateral root and nodule development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mapping-by-sequencing; complementation; promoter-GUS; mutant sphingolipidomics; RT-qPCR; microscopy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bradyrhizobium ORS278 inoculation; roots grown in buffered nodulation medium with 0.5 or 5 mM KNO3
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SPT complex (serine palmitoyltransferase), inferred from ORM function stated in paper
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Mapping-by-sequencing, NCBI BioProject PRJNA727694; sphingolipidomics by UPLC-ESI-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AeORM1 promoter (~2.1 kb); proAeORM1-GUS reporter; ProAeORM1:AeORM1 complementation construct
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize sphingolipid homeostasis for legume root architecture and nodulation, but downregulation risks severe nodulation defects and defense-like activation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes aborted nodules, defense-like phenolic accumulation, impaired bacteroid differentiation, reduced nitrogen fixation, and shorter lateral roots
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AeORM1 is a negative regulator of sphingolipid biosynthesis rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or TF; mycorrhization was not impaired in orm1 mutants, suggesting symbiosis-specific or redundant roles
